title: Shapes Galore!
description: More shapes and their parameters. Focus: reading code and understanding what each number controls.

flashcards.definition: An oval is like a stretched circle. It usually needs position + width + height.
A line is defined by two points: (x1, y1) and (x2, y2).
A polygon is a shape with multiple corners (points).
Text can be drawn on the canvas (useful for titles and instructions).

quiz.prompt: A line uses two points. How many numbers does that usually require?
quiz.feedbackCorrect: Correct: a line uses 2 points → 4 numbers.
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: A line uses (x1,y1) and (x2,y2), so 4 numbers.

quiz.prompt: Which shape is best described as a 'stretched circle'?
quiz.feedbackCorrect: Correct: an oval is a stretched circle.
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: An oval/ellipse is like a stretched circle.
